Florencia Hero Review: The Snow Warden of Winter - Empires and Puzzles

Is Florencia the ice-cold silencer your Yellow stack needs, or just another snowdrift in the Winter family? We break down her 'never-miss' AoE, that tricky highest-HP targeting, and whether her frost-biting minions are worth the summoning materials.
Florencia: The Snowman Who Silences the Crowd
Florencia, The Snow Warden: Silence is Golden (And Yellow)

Florencia arrives as part of the 2025 Winter Event lineup, bringing a lantern to light the way and a heavy hand to silence the opposition. She is a 5-star Holy (Yellow) Cleric who operates at Average speed. Visually, she is a charming, bundled-up snow-creature, but don't let the cozy vibes fool you—her kit is built for disruption and team sustainability. While many players might overlook seasonal heroes in favor of the latest shiny tower units, Florencia offers a reliable mix of guaranteed damage and control. She's basically what happens if Frosty the Snowman decided to take up a career in law enforcement.

The "Snow Warden" design is a delightful pivot from the usual edgy warriors and gods. Florencia is a sturdy, round snow-person clad in a warm, golden-yellow cloak, holding a lantern that presumably guides lost souls (or just highlights who she's about to smack). The art style captures the whimsy of the Winter event while maintaining a sense of magical authority. It’s hard to be intimidated by a nose made of a carrot, but when that carrot is backed by 1300+ attack power, you learn to respect the produce.
Florencia enters the arena with stats that are perfectly aligned with the late 2025 meta. With an
, she is well-balanced, though slightly leaning towards offense. Compared to her contemporaries like

(1320 Atk), she holds her own comfortably. She is not a glass cannon, nor is she a meat shield; she sits in that "bruiser" sweet spot where she can take a hit and dish one out. Her 2263 Health is standard for the modern 5-star tier, ensuring she doesn't melt at the first sign of fire.

For an Average speed hero, this is a respectable multiplier. It's enough to soften up an entire team significantly, especially with her high base attack.

This is the tactical hook. It completely shuts down one enemy. The duration (4 turns) is generous. However, targeting the "highest HP" enemy is a double-edged sword. It often misses the tank (who has likely taken tile damage) and targets a flank or wing.
The 20% HP/Attack minions add a layer of meat shields to your team, increasing survivability and adding chip damage.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family has evolved, and Florencia benefits from these modern updates.
The Winter Family bonus boosts the stats of the minions. While nice, the real power lies in the passive synergy.
This is a sneaky source of damage. Each hit from her minions inflicts
over 4 turns. Since she summons minions for

, if you can keep them alive, the DoT (Damage over Time) pressure ramps up quickly. Just be careful against heroes that benefit from receiving status ailments or specific elemental damage (looking at you, Untold Tales 2 Frost family).
At Level 90, she gains a raw attack boost at the start of battle. This synergizes perfectly with her "Hit All" special, ensuring her opening salvo hits as hard as possible.

The Winter event has a history of giving us iconic tanks (Krampus, Mother North) and annoying minion makers (Pengi). Florencia fits into the "Minion Maker + Damage" archetype but adds a modern control element.

Her stats are fresh off the press. She is not suffering from "old hero syndrome." Her 345% hit to all is strong, considering older average heroes did 280-300% without the added utility of silence and minions.

She is a versatile hero who fits into many team compositions. She isn't a "must-have" that breaks the game like some overpowered Goblins, but she is a solid A-tier hero who will win you wars and raids.

"Never Misses" guarantees value against Dodge heroes.
She provides both offensive pressure (AoE + DoT) and defensive utility (Silence + Minions).
The 4-turn Silence is long enough to prevent a healer or reviver in the wing from ruining your match.
The Frost DoT from minions adds up silently and deadly.
You cannot choose who gets silenced. If the highest HP enemy is a harmless passive tank or a hero with Monk/Cleric resist, the silence might be wasted.
In a Rush meta, she's great, but in standard play, she can be outpaced by Very Fast/Fast threats.

Being Average speed makes her a monster in Very Fast modes. She charges in 6 tiles and shuts down key threats.
The "Never Miss" is crucial against bosses with high dodge or blind mechanics.
Minions provide "healing" (via HP buffer) in a mode where healing is banned, and her damage is significant.
Great for cracking stall teams where you need to silence a wing healer.
Florencia - Pengi - Mother North - Freya - Odhinn
Florencia provides minions for Pengi to launch. Her silence protects the team while the minion army builds up.
Rhys boosts tiles, Florencia and Ogima provide devastating AoE damage.

If you pull Florencia, level her up immediately. She is a carry hero for map stages (minions keep you alive) and a strong raider. Focus on getting her special skill to 8/8 to maximize the silence duration.
She is a "luxury utility" pick. If you have

is unique. She is an excellent counter-pick for teams running annoying wing heroes like

rated her a "7 out of 10," feeling she was a bit underwhelming for a featured seasonal hero.

Florencia is a solid, dependable hero who brings a warm lantern to a cold fight. While she might not have the flashy "delete button" potential of some recent releases, her combination of guaranteed hits, silencing capability, and team-wide minion support makes her a valuable asset in any roster. She’s the kind of hero you might underestimate until she silences your healer for 4 turns and slowly buries you in snowballs.
A strong addition to the Holy roster, particularly valuable for Rush Attack wars and countering Dodge-heavy defenses.
